plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to exit all leveraged ETFs, as per conservative strategy and their stop breaches.
Increase QQQI to 25% and QQQ to 20% for stable income and broad market exposure.
Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single name concentration.
This plan significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for capital preservation.
Maintain a cash buffer of ~15-20% by reducing overall exposure.
conservative.read: This conservative approach targets around 1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation and low drawdown. It significantly reduces overall portfolio risk by cutting leveraged ETFs and lowering concentration, but may limit upside potential.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL due to its stop breach and to reduce overall leverage.
Trim SPAL to 2% to reduce leveraged ETF exposure while maintaining a small position.
Slightly trim CRWD to 12% to manage single name concentration.
Reduce SMH to 10% given its downtrend status.
Buy AMD at 5% as a strong scanner setup to add quality tech exposure.
balanced.read: This balanced approach aims for approximately 2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces leverage and concentration while blending broad index and quality tech, accepting moderate drawdown potential.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL due to its stop breach; reallocate capital to stronger setups.
Maintain SPAL at 2% for continued leveraged exposure.
Add to CRWD, increasing to 18%, to press a winning position.
Buy AMD at 10% as a high-scoring scanner setup, increasing tech concentration.
Trim SMH to 8% to reduce exposure to a downtrending sector.
aggressive.read: This aggressive growth approach targets around 4% per month by leaning into high-scoring setups and maintaining leveraged exposure. This strategy carries significantly higher risk, including greater drawdown potential and daily decay from leveraged ETFs, requiring strict stop discipline.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to completely exit all leveraged ETFs.
Sell CRWD and SMH to eliminate high-beta single names and sectors.
Significantly add to QQQI, increasing to 40%, to prioritize income.
This plan focuses on rotating into income and broad index ETFs for steady distributions.
Maintain a cash buffer by reducing overall exposure to volatile assets.
income.read: This income-focused approach targets around 1% per month, prioritizing steady monthly distributions and low drawdown. While aiming for cash-like income, distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in a selloff, so it is not a savings account.
